title: Berlin Marathon 2026: Quick Summary
items: **The Berlin Marathon 2026 is on Sunday 27 September 2026.** Runners start at 09:15, wheelchair racers and handbikes at around 08:50, and the start and finish are both on Straße des 17. Juni between the Brandenburg Gate and the Kleiner Stern.

**The roads close hours before any runner arrives.** Unter den Linden goes from about 06:00, Mitte and the west from around 07:45, Kreuzberg from 08:00, Schöneberg from 08:15. Nothing meaningful reopens until the afternoon.

**The east comes back first.** Following the 2025 pattern, Friedrichshain and Kreuzberg reopen around 14:00, the south around 16:30, Mitte and the west around 17:30, and Unter den Linden last at about 19:00.

**The U-Bahn and S-Bahn run normally and go under the course.** That is how you cross it. Buses and trams keep running but get diverted, and the diversions shift through the day, so trains are the reliable layer.

**Saturday 26 September is affected too.** The inline skating marathon uses the full course through the afternoon, and Unter den Linden between Pariser Platz and Friedrichstraße was closed 07:00 to 18:00 in 2025.

**The Tiergarten start area is fenced for roughly two weeks.** In 2025 Straße des 17. Juni was closed from 13 September until 24 September, so the Victory Column and the central axis are surrounded by build-up well before race day.
**Best place to watch is west and late.** The Memorial Church at km 35 and Potsdamer Platz at km 38.5 are far less crowded than the Reichstag at km 6.5 or the Brandenburg Gate finish.

**Exact 2026 closure times are published in September** by the organiser and Berlin’s traffic information centre. Use the pattern above to plan the shape of the day, then check the official map in the week before you travel.
